speaker: SLY
text: I’ll feeze you, in faith.

speaker: HOSTESS
text: A pair of stocks, you rogue!

speaker: SLY
text: You’re a baggage! The Slys are no rogues. Look

speaker: SLY
text: in the chronicles. We came in with Richard Conqueror.

speaker: SLY
text: Therefore, paucas pallabris, let the world

speaker: SLY
text: slide. Sessa!

speaker: HOSTESS
text: You will not pay for the glasses you have

speaker: HOSTESS
text: burst?

speaker: SLY
text: No, not a denier. Go, by Saint Jeronimy! Go to

speaker: SLY
text: thy cold bed and warm thee.

speaker: HOSTESS
text: I know my remedy. I must go fetch the

speaker: HOSTESS
text: headborough.

speaker: SLY
text: Third, or fourth, or fifth borough, I’ll answer him

speaker: SLY
text: by law. I’ll not budge an inch, boy. Let him come,

speaker: SLY
text: and kindly.

speaker: LORD
text: Huntsman, I charge thee tender well my hounds.

speaker: LORD
text: Breathe Merriman (the poor cur is embossed)

speaker: LORD
text: And couple Clowder with the deep-mouthed brach.

speaker: LORD
text: Saw’st thou not, boy, how Silver made it good

speaker: LORD
text: At the hedge corner, in the coldest fault?

speaker: LORD
text: I would not lose the dog for twenty pound!

speaker:
FIRST
               
              HUNTSMAN
text: Why, Bellman is as good as he, my lord.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              HUNTSMAN
text: He cried upon it at the merest loss,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              HUNTSMAN
text: And twice today picked out the dullest scent.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              HUNTSMAN
text: Trust me, I take him for the better dog.

speaker: LORD
text: Thou art a fool. If Echo were as fleet,

speaker: LORD
text: I would esteem him worth a dozen such.

speaker: LORD
text: But sup them well, and look unto them all.

speaker: LORD
text: Tomorrow I intend to hunt again.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              HUNTSMAN
text: I will, my lord.

speaker: LORD
text: What’s here? One dead, or drunk? See doth he breathe.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              HUNTSMAN
text: He breathes, my lord. Were he not warmed with ale,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              HUNTSMAN
text: This were a bed but cold to sleep so soundly.

speaker: LORD
text: O monstrous beast, how like a swine he lies!

speaker: LORD
text: Grim death, how foul and loathsome is thine image!

speaker: LORD
text: Sirs, I will practice on this drunken man.

speaker: LORD
text: What think you, if he were conveyed to bed,

speaker: LORD
text: Wrapped in sweet clothes, rings put upon his fingers,

speaker: LORD
text: A most delicious banquet by his bed,

speaker: LORD
text: And brave attendants near him when he wakes,

speaker: LORD
text: Would not the beggar then forget himself?

speaker:
THIRD
               
              HUNTSMAN
text: Believe me, lord, I think he cannot choose.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              HUNTSMAN
text: It would seem strange unto him when he waked.

speaker: LORD
text: Even as a flatt’ring dream or worthless fancy.

speaker: LORD
text: Then take him up, and manage well the jest.

speaker: LORD
text: Carry him gently to my fairest chamber,

speaker: LORD
text: And hang it round with all my wanton pictures;

speaker: LORD
text: Balm his foul head in warm distillèd waters,

speaker: LORD
text: And burn sweet wood to make the lodging sweet;

speaker: LORD
text: Procure me music ready when he wakes

speaker: LORD
text: To make a dulcet and a heavenly sound.

speaker: LORD
text: And if he chance to speak, be ready straight

speaker: LORD
text: And, with a low, submissive reverence,

speaker: LORD
text: Say “What is it your Honor will command?”

speaker: LORD
text: Let one attend him with a silver basin

speaker: LORD
text: Full of rosewater and bestrewed with flowers,

speaker: LORD
text: Another bear the ewer, the third a diaper,

speaker: LORD
text: And say “Will ’t please your Lordship cool your hands?”

speaker: LORD
text: Someone be ready with a costly suit,

speaker: LORD
text: And ask him what apparel he will wear.

speaker: LORD
text: Another tell him of his hounds and horse,

speaker: LORD
text: And that his lady mourns at his disease.

speaker: LORD
text: Persuade him that he hath been lunatic,

speaker: LORD
text: And when he says he is, say that he dreams,

speaker: LORD
text: For he is nothing but a mighty lord.

speaker: LORD
text: This do, and do it kindly, gentle sirs.

speaker: LORD
text: It will be pastime passing excellent

speaker: LORD
text: If it be husbanded with modesty.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              HUNTSMAN
text: My lord, I warrant you we will play our part

speaker:
THIRD
               
              HUNTSMAN
text: As he shall think by our true diligence

speaker:
THIRD
               
              HUNTSMAN
text: He is no less than what we say he is.

speaker: LORD
text: Take him up gently, and to bed with him,

speaker: LORD
text: And each one to his office when he wakes.

speaker: LORD
text: Sirrah, go see what trumpet ’tis that sounds.

speaker: LORD
text: Belike some noble gentleman that means

speaker: LORD
text: (Traveling some journey) to repose him here.

speaker: LORD
text: How now? Who is it?

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: An ’t please your Honor, players

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: That offer service to your Lordship.

speaker: LORD
text: Bid them come near.

speaker: LORD
text: Now, fellows, you are welcome.

speaker: PLAYERS
text: We thank your Honor.

speaker: LORD
text: Do you intend to stay with me tonight?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              PLAYER
text: So please your Lordship to accept our duty.

speaker: LORD
text: With all my heart. This fellow I remember

speaker: LORD
text: Since once he played a farmer’s eldest son.—

speaker: LORD
text: ’Twas where you wooed the gentlewoman so well.

speaker: LORD
text: I have forgot your name, but sure that part

speaker: LORD
text: Was aptly fitted and naturally performed.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              PLAYER
text: I think ’twas Soto that your Honor means.

speaker: LORD
text: ’Tis very true. Thou didst it excellent.

speaker: LORD
text: Well, you are come to me in happy time,

speaker: LORD
text: The rather for I have some sport in hand

speaker: LORD
text: Wherein your cunning can assist me much.

speaker: LORD
text: There is a lord will hear you play tonight;

speaker: LORD
text: But I am doubtful of your modesties,

speaker: LORD
text: Lest, over-eying of his odd behavior

speaker: LORD
text: (For yet his Honor never heard a play),

speaker: LORD
text: You break into some merry passion,

speaker: LORD
text: And so offend him. For I tell you, sirs,

speaker: LORD
text: If you should smile, he grows impatient.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              PLAYER
text: Fear not, my lord, we can contain ourselves

speaker:
FIRST
               
              PLAYER
text: Were he the veriest antic in the world.

speaker: LORD
text: Go, sirrah, take them to the buttery

speaker: LORD
text: And give them friendly welcome every one.

speaker: LORD
text: Let them want nothing that my house affords.

speaker: LORD
text: Sirrah, go you to Bartholomew, my page,

speaker: LORD
text: And see him dressed in all suits like a lady.

speaker: LORD
text: That done, conduct him to the drunkard’s chamber,

speaker: LORD
text: And call him “Madam,” do him obeisance.

speaker: LORD
text: Tell him from me, as he will win my love,

speaker: LORD
text: He bear himself with honorable action,

speaker: LORD
text: Such as he hath observed in noble ladies

speaker: LORD
text: Unto their lords, by them accomplishèd.

speaker: LORD
text: Such duty to the drunkard let him do

speaker: LORD
text: With soft low tongue and lowly courtesy,

speaker: LORD
text: And say “What is ’t your Honor will command,

speaker: LORD
text: Wherein your lady and your humble wife

speaker: LORD
text: May show her duty and make known her love?”

speaker: LORD
text: And then with kind embracements, tempting kisses,

speaker: LORD
text: And with declining head into his bosom,

speaker: LORD
text: Bid him shed tears, as being overjoyed

speaker: LORD
text: To see her noble lord restored to health,

speaker: LORD
text: Who, for this seven years, hath esteemed him

speaker: LORD
text: No better than a poor and loathsome beggar.

speaker: LORD
text: And if the boy have not a woman’s gift

speaker: LORD
text: To rain a shower of commanded tears,

speaker: LORD
text: An onion will do well for such a shift,

speaker: LORD
text: Which (in a napkin being close conveyed)

speaker: LORD
text: Shall in despite enforce a watery eye.

speaker: LORD
text: See this dispatched with all the haste thou canst.

speaker: LORD
text: Anon I’ll give thee more instructions.

speaker: LORD
text: I know the boy will well usurp the grace,

speaker: LORD
text: Voice, gait, and action of a gentlewoman.

speaker: LORD
text: I long to hear him call the drunkard “husband”!

speaker: LORD
text: And how my men will stay themselves from laughter

speaker: LORD
text: When they do homage to this simple peasant,

speaker: LORD
text: I’ll in to counsel them. Haply my presence

speaker: LORD
text: May well abate the over-merry spleen

speaker: LORD
text: Which otherwise would grow into extremes.

speaker: SLY
text: For God’s sake, a pot of small ale.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Will ’t please your Lord drink a cup of sack?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Will ’t please your Honor taste of these conserves?

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: What raiment will your Honor wear today?

speaker: SLY
text: I am Christophero Sly! Call not me “Honor” nor

speaker: SLY
text: “Lordship.” I ne’er drank sack in my life. An if you

speaker: SLY
text: give me any conserves, give me conserves of beef.

speaker: SLY
text: Ne’er ask me what raiment I’ll wear, for I have no

speaker: SLY
text: more doublets than backs, no more stockings than

speaker: SLY
text: legs, nor no more shoes than feet, nay sometime

speaker: SLY
text: more feet than shoes, or such shoes as my toes look

speaker: SLY
text: through the over-leather.

speaker: LORD
text: Heaven cease this idle humor in your Honor!

speaker: LORD
text: O, that a mighty man of such descent,

speaker: LORD
text: Of such possessions, and so high esteem

speaker: LORD
text: Should be infusèd with so foul a spirit!

speaker: SLY
text: What, would you make me mad? Am not I Christopher

speaker: SLY
text: Sly, old Sly’s son of Burton Heath, by birth a

speaker: SLY
text: peddler, by education a cardmaker, by transmutation

speaker: SLY
text: a bearherd, and now by present profession a

speaker: SLY
text: tinker? Ask Marian Hacket, the fat alewife of Wincot,

speaker: SLY
text: if she know me not! If she say I am not fourteen

speaker: SLY
text: pence on the score for sheer ale, score me up for the

speaker: SLY
text: lying’st knave in Christendom. What, I am not

speaker: SLY
text: bestraught! Here’s—

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: O, this it is that makes your lady mourn.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: O, this is it that makes your servants droop.

speaker: LORD
text: Hence comes it that your kindred shuns your house,

speaker: LORD
text: As beaten hence by your strange lunacy.

speaker: LORD
text: O noble lord, bethink thee of thy birth,

speaker: LORD
text: Call home thy ancient thoughts from banishment,

speaker: LORD
text: And banish hence these abject lowly dreams.

speaker: LORD
text: Look how thy servants do attend on thee,

speaker: LORD
text: Each in his office ready at thy beck.

speaker: LORD
text: Wilt thou have music? Hark, Apollo plays,

speaker: LORD
text: And twenty cagèd nightingales do sing.

speaker: LORD
text: Or wilt thou sleep? We’ll have thee to a couch

speaker: LORD
text: Softer and sweeter than the lustful bed

speaker: LORD
text: On purpose trimmed up for Semiramis.

speaker: LORD
text: Say thou wilt walk, we will bestrew the ground.

speaker: LORD
text: Or wilt thou ride? Thy horses shall be trapped,

speaker: LORD
text: Their harness studded all with gold and pearl.

speaker: LORD
text: Dost thou love hawking? Thou hast hawks will soar

speaker: LORD
text: Above the morning lark. Or wilt thou hunt?

speaker: LORD
text: Thy hounds shall make the welkin answer them

speaker: LORD
text: And fetch shrill echoes from the hollow earth.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Say thou wilt course. Thy greyhounds are as swift

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: As breathèd stags, ay, fleeter than the roe.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Dost thou love pictures? We will fetch thee straight

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Adonis painted by a running brook,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: And Cytherea all in sedges hid,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Which seem to move and wanton with her breath,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Even as the waving sedges play with wind.

speaker: LORD
text: We’ll show thee Io as she was a maid

speaker: LORD
text: And how she was beguilèd and surprised,

speaker: LORD
text: As lively painted as the deed was done.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Or Daphne roaming through a thorny wood,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Scratching her legs that one shall swear she bleeds,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: And at that sight shall sad Apollo weep,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: So workmanly the blood and tears are drawn.

speaker: LORD
text: Thou art a lord, and nothing but a lord;

speaker: LORD
text: Thou hast a lady far more beautiful

speaker: LORD
text: Than any woman in this waning age.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: And till the tears that she hath shed for thee

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Like envious floods o’errun her lovely face,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: She was the fairest creature in the world—

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: And yet she is inferior to none.

speaker: SLY
text: Am I a lord, and have I such a lady?

speaker: SLY
text: Or do I dream? Or have I dreamed till now?

speaker: SLY
text: I do not sleep: I see, I hear, I speak,

speaker: SLY
text: I smell sweet savors, and I feel soft things.

speaker: SLY
text: Upon my life, I am a lord indeed

speaker: SLY
text: And not a tinker, nor Christopher Sly.

speaker: SLY
text: Well, bring our lady hither to our sight,

speaker: SLY
text: And once again a pot o’ the smallest ale.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Will ’t please your Mightiness to wash your hands?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: O, how we joy to see your wit restored!

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: O, that once more you knew but what you are!

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: These fifteen years you have been in a dream,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Or, when you waked, so waked as if you slept.

speaker: SLY
text: These fifteen years! By my fay, a goodly nap.

speaker: SLY
text: But did I never speak of all that time?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Oh, yes, my lord, but very idle words.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: For though you lay here in this goodly chamber,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Yet would you say you were beaten out of door,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: And rail upon the hostess of the house,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: And say you would present her at the leet

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Because she brought stone jugs and no sealed quarts.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Sometimes you would call out for Cicely Hacket.

speaker: SLY
text: Ay, the woman’s maid of the house.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Why, sir, you know no house, nor no such maid,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Nor no such men as you have reckoned up,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: As Stephen Sly and old John Naps of Greete,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: And Peter Turph and Henry Pimpernell,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: And twenty more such names and men as these,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Which never were, nor no man ever saw.

speaker: SLY
text: Now, Lord be thanked for my good amends!

speaker: ALL
text: Amen.

speaker: SLY
text: I thank thee. Thou shalt not lose by it.

speaker: PAGE
text: How fares my noble lord?

speaker: SLY
text: Marry, I fare well, for here is cheer enough.

speaker: SLY
text: Where is my wife?

speaker: PAGE
text: Here, noble lord. What is thy will with her?

speaker: SLY
text: Are you my wife, and will not call me “husband”?

speaker: SLY
text: My men should call me “lord.” I am your goodman.

speaker: PAGE
text: My husband and my lord, my lord and husband,

speaker: PAGE
text: I am your wife in all obedience.

speaker: SLY
text: I know it well.—What must I call her?

speaker: LORD
text: “Madam.”

speaker: SLY
text: “Alice Madam,” or “Joan Madam”?

speaker: LORD
text: “Madam,” and nothing else. So lords call ladies.

speaker: SLY
text: Madam wife, they say that I have dreamed

speaker: SLY
text: And slept above some fifteen year or more.

speaker: PAGE
text: Ay, and the time seems thirty unto me,

speaker: PAGE
text: Being all this time abandoned from your bed.

speaker: SLY
text: ’Tis much.—Servants, leave me and her alone.—

speaker: SLY
text: Madam, undress you, and come now to bed.

speaker: PAGE
text: Thrice noble lord, let me entreat of you

speaker: PAGE
text: To pardon me yet for a night or two;

speaker: PAGE
text: Or if not so, until the sun be set.

speaker: PAGE
text: For your physicians have expressly charged,

speaker: PAGE
text: In peril to incur your former malady,

speaker: PAGE
text: That I should yet absent me from your bed.

speaker: PAGE
text: I hope this reason stands for my excuse.

speaker: SLY
text: Ay, it stands so that I may hardly tarry so long; but

speaker: SLY
text: I would be loath to fall into my dreams again. I will

speaker: SLY
text: therefore tarry in despite of the flesh and the

speaker: SLY
text: blood.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Your Honor’s players, hearing your amendment,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Are come to play a pleasant comedy,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: For so your doctors hold it very meet,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Seeing too much sadness hath congealed your blood,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: And melancholy is the nurse of frenzy.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Therefore they thought it good you hear a play

speaker: MESSENGER
text: And frame your mind to mirth and merriment,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Which bars a thousand harms and lengthens life.

speaker: SLY
text: Marry, I will. Let them play it.

speaker: SLY
text: Is not a comonty a Christmas gambold or a tumbling

speaker: SLY
text: trick?

speaker: PAGE
text: No, my good lord, it is more pleasing stuff.

speaker: SLY
text: What, household stuff?

speaker: PAGE
text: It is a kind of history.

speaker: SLY
text: Well, we’ll see ’t. Come, madam wife, sit by my

speaker: SLY
text: side, and let the world slip. We shall ne’er be

speaker: SLY
text: younger.
